question_text: Relay cropping in agronomy is most accurately defined by which specific timing pattern of two crops in the same field?
explanation: Relay cropping is the practice of sowing a second crop into a standing first crop before the first crop is harvested, like passing a relay baton. A classic Indian example is paira cropping where lathyrus or lentil seed is broadcast into standing transplanted rice about a fortnight before its harvest, so the legume utilises residual soil moisture after rice is removed.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Both crops sown together at same time in alternate rows
Second crop sown in standing first crop before its harvest
Second crop sown only one year after the first crop
Same crop grown repeatedly on the same field for years

elimination_hints.hint: Sowing together in rows is intercropping, not relay
One-year gap is sequence cropping, not relay
Repeated same crop is monocropping, not relay

question_text: Ratoon cropping in sugarcane is most accurately described by which specific practice carried out after harvest of the main crop?
explanation: Ratooning is the practice of allowing a new crop to grow from the underground stubble (basal nodes) of the harvested previous crop, without planting fresh seed. Sugarcane and sorghum are commonly ratooned. The ratoon crop saves seed cost, land preparation cost, and matures earlier, but yield typically declines slightly compared to the planted (plant) crop.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Sowing fresh seeds of sugarcane after deep ploughing
Planting tissue-cultured plantlets in place of setts
Allowing new crop to grow from stubble of harvested cane
Burning the stubble field completely before next sowing

elimination_hints.hint: Fresh seed sowing is plant crop, not ratoon
Tissue-cultured plantlets are an alternative to ratooning
Burning destroys stubble, so no ratoon is possible

question_text: Alley cropping or hedgerow intercropping is best described as which specific spatial combination of woody and arable components?
explanation: Alley cropping is an agroforestry practice in which arable food crops are grown in alleys (strips) between rows of woody perennial trees or shrubs, often nitrogen-fixing species like Leucaena leucocephala or Gliricidia. The trees are pruned periodically; their leaves serve as mulch or green manure and improve soil fertility, while the food crop is grown in the alleys.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Trees and food crops grown in completely separate fields
Food crops grown in alleys between rows of woody perennial trees
Two food crops grown together without any tree in field
Trees grown only along boundary fences of a crop field

elimination_hints.hint: Separate fields is not alley cropping
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing alley cropping with land use planning

elimination_hints.hint: Two crops without tree is intercropping, not alley cropping
Boundary planting alone is windbreak, not alley cropping
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing boundary trees with hedgerow intercropping

question_text: Dryland agriculture in India is most correctly defined as crop production in regions with which specific annual rainfall range?
explanation: Dryland agriculture is conventionally defined as crop production in areas receiving 500-750 mm of rainfall annually with no irrigation. Areas below 500 mm are termed arid, while areas above 1150 mm are considered rainfed. Drylands cover about 60 percent of India's net cultivated area, and rely on practices like in-situ moisture conservation, mulching and drought-tolerant crops.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Areas receiving less than 250 mm rainfall annually only
Areas receiving 500 to 750 mm of rainfall annually
Areas receiving above 2000 mm of rainfall annually
Areas receiving zero rainfall in the entire year normally

elimination_hints.hint: Below 250 mm is desert, beyond dryland classification
Above 2000 mm is high-rainfall, not dryland
Zero rainfall would be barren desert

question_text: Land Equivalent Ratio (LER) of an intercropping system is most correctly defined by which specific quantitative formula?
explanation: Land Equivalent Ratio is calculated as the sum of the ratios of intercrop yield divided by sole-crop yield for each component crop. Mathematically, LER = (Y_a / S_a) + (Y_b / S_b), where Y is intercrop yield and S is sole-crop yield. An LER greater than 1 means the intercrop is more productive than sole crops occupying the same land separately.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Sum of ratios of intercrop yield to sole-crop yield for each component
Total yield of intercrop divided by total area of the field used
Sole-crop yield of cereal divided by sole-crop yield of legume
Cropping intensity divided by total irrigated area on the farm

elimination_hints.hint: Yield per area is land productivity, not LER
Ratio of two sole crops is not the LER concept
This is unrelated to intercropping evaluation

question_text: Crop rotation in agronomy is most correctly defined by which specific principle of growing crops over time on a field?
explanation: Crop rotation is the practice of growing different crops in a planned succession on the same field over a cycle of two or more years. A common rotation includes a cereal followed by a legume followed by a cash crop. Rotation breaks pest and disease cycles, replenishes nitrogen through legume inclusion, manages weeds, and maintains soil fertility better than continuous monoculture.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Growing the same crop on the same field for many years
Growing two crops together in distinct row patterns in field
Growing different crops in planned succession over multiple years
Leaving the field uncultivated for many consecutive years

elimination_hints.hint: Same crop year after year is monoculture
Two crops together is intercropping, not rotation
Long fallow is not crop rotation by definition

question_text: Inclusion of a leguminous crop in the rotation cycle benefits the next cereal crop primarily through which specific mechanism?
explanation: Legumes have root-nodule bacteria (Rhizobium) that fix atmospheric nitrogen, leaving 30-60 kg of residual nitrogen per hectare in the soil after harvest. The next cereal crop in the rotation directly benefits from this residual nitrogen, requiring less external fertiliser. Hence cereal-legume rotations like maize-cowpea or wheat-chickpea are sustainable and economical.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: By depleting soil moisture for the next cereal crop
By fixing atmospheric nitrogen via Rhizobium nodules
By increasing the soil pH to highly alkaline range
By creating dense canopy that smothers weeds completely

elimination_hints.hint: Moisture depletion is harmful, not beneficial
Legumes do not strongly alkalinise soil
Weed smothering is incidental, not the main benefit to cereal

question_text: Organic farming as defined in India strictly excludes which specific class of agricultural inputs from being used by the farmer?
explanation: Organic farming, certified under the National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP), strictly excludes synthetic chemical fertilisers, synthetic pesticides, herbicides and genetically modified seeds. It permits use of compost, farmyard manure, vermicompost, green manure, biofertilisers like Rhizobium, Azotobacter, biopesticides like neem and Trichoderma, and traditional non-GM seeds.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Compost and farmyard manure prepared on the farm itself
Synthetic chemical fertilisers, pesticides, herbicides and GM seeds
Biofertilisers like Rhizobium and Azotobacter cultures
Botanical pesticides like neem and Pongamia oil sprays

elimination_hints.hint: FYM and compost are central to organic farming
Biofertilisers are encouraged in organic farming
Botanical pesticides are permitted in organic systems

question_text: Sikkim achieved a major Indian agricultural milestone in 2016 by becoming the first state in India to do which specific thing?
explanation: Sikkim became the first fully organic state of India in 2016, having converted all its 76,000 hectares of agricultural land to certified organic production over more than a decade through a phased ban on chemical fertilisers and pesticides. This pioneering effort earned Sikkim the FAO's Future Policy Gold Award in 2018 for sustainable food and agriculture policies.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: First state to ban all kharif rice cultivation completely
First state to grow only genetically modified crops in fields
First fully organic state with all farmland certified organic
First state to abolish minimum support price for crops

elimination_hints.hint: No state has banned rice cultivation
GM crops are restricted in India and Sikkim is organic, not GM
MSP is a national policy, not state abolished

question_text: An intercropping experiment shows yields of crop A as 2.4 t/ha (sole 4 t/ha) and crop B as 1.5 t/ha (sole 2.5 t/ha). What is the LER value?
explanation: Land Equivalent Ratio = (Y_a / S_a) + (Y_b / S_b) = (2.4 / 4) + (1.5 / 2.5) = 0.60 + 0.60 = 1.20. An LER value of 1.20 indicates that the intercropping system is 20 percent more productive than growing the two crops separately on the same total area. Thus the system shows positive yield advantage over sole cropping.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: 0.80 indicating the intercrop is poorer than sole cropping
1.20 indicating 20 percent yield advantage of intercropping
1.60 indicating 60 percent yield advantage of intercropping
2.00 indicating exactly double productivity of intercropping

elimination_hints.hint: 0.60 + 0.60 cannot equal 0.80
0.60 + 0.60 cannot equal 1.60
Sum of two 0.60 values cannot reach 2.00

question_text: Match the cropping practices in Column A with the correct distinguishing feature given in Column B for each system.
explanation: Sequence cropping grows two crops one after another in same field per year; relay cropping sows the second crop in standing first crop before harvest; ratoon cropping uses regrowth from stubble; and alley cropping grows arable crops between rows of perennial woody trees.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

column_b.text: Crops grown in alleys between rows of woody trees
Regrowth of new crop from stubble of the harvested crop
Second crop sown in standing first crop before harvest
Two crops one after another in same field per year

elimination_hints.hint: Sequence cropping is not alley cropping
Sequence cropping is not ratoon
Sequence cropping is not relay

question_text: Match the rainfall categories in Column A with their correct annual rainfall ranges and farming categories in Column B.
explanation: Arid regions receive less than 500 mm rainfall and rely on extensive grazing or drought-resistant millets; dryland regions receive 500-750 mm; rainfed regions receive 750-1150 mm; and humid regions receive above 1150 mm rainfall annually with ample water for intensive cropping. Each category needs distinct crop and management strategies.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

elimination_hints.hint: Arid is below 500 mm, not above 1150 mm
Arid is not 500-750 mm, that is dryland
Arid does not receive 750-1150 mm rainfall

question_text: Match the agronomic management approaches in Column A with their correct distinguishing characteristics described in Column B.
explanation: Conventional farming uses synthetic inputs and high-yielding varieties; organic farming excludes synthetic inputs and uses compost and biofertilisers; precision agriculture applies inputs site-specifically guided by GPS sensors; and integrated farming combines crops, livestock and other enterprises in a complementary system on the same farm.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

column_b.text: Combining crops, livestock and other enterprises complementarily
Site-specific input use guided by GPS and sensors
Excludes synthetic inputs; uses compost and biofertilisers
Uses synthetic inputs and high-yielding varieties widely

elimination_hints.hint: Conventional farming is not integrated farming
Conventional farming uses synthetic inputs, not organic
Conventional is not precision agriculture

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about cereal-legume crop rotation carefully and choose the most correct option below.
explanation: Both statements are true and the reason correctly explains the assertion. The legume crop in the rotation hosts Rhizobium bacteria in its root nodules, which fix atmospheric nitrogen and enrich the soil with 30-60 kg N per hectare. The succeeding cereal crop directly utilises this residual nitrogen, requiring less synthetic urea and thus reducing fertiliser cost while maintaining yield.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture
assertion: Cereal-legume rotation reduces the synthetic fertiliser nitrogen requirement of the cereal crop in the rotation.
reason: Legumes fix atmospheric nitrogen via Rhizobium and leave residual nitrogen in soil after harvest.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: Residual N from legume directly explains lower fertiliser need
Rhizobium nitrogen fixation is well established
Reduced cereal N requirement after legume is well documented

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about ratoon cropping in sugarcane carefully and choose the most appropriate option from below.
explanation: Both statements are true and the reason correctly explains the assertion. Ratoon crops use the existing root system and stubble of the harvested cane, eliminating the cost of fresh seed setts (which is 10-15 percent of total cost) and the cost of land preparation and planting labour. Hence ratoon cane is much cheaper to raise than a freshly planted (plant) crop.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture
assertion: Ratoon sugarcane crop is significantly cheaper to raise than freshly planted plant cane crop.
reason: Ratoon crop uses existing stubble and root system, saving seed-sett cost and land-preparation cost.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: Saving in seed and tillage cost is the very reason ratoon is cheaper
Stubble use saving costs is well established
Lower ratoon cost is a well-known cane economics fact

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about precision agriculture in modern farms carefully and choose the option which best fits.
explanation: The assertion is true but the reason is false. Precision agriculture is more, not less, input efficient than uniform broadcast application, because GPS-guided variable-rate technology applies fertiliser, water and pesticides exactly where and in the amount needed by each plot. Hence precision agriculture reduces input wastage and environmental impact, contrary to the false reason given.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture
assertion: Precision agriculture using GPS-guided variable-rate input application improves input-use efficiency.
reason: Precision agriculture uses much more fertiliser and water uniformly across the field than conventional methods.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: Precision agriculture uses less, not more inputs uniformly
The reason itself is conceptually false
Improved input efficiency by precision agriculture is well established

question_text: Arrange the following years and policy events related to organic farming in India in correct chronological order from earliest.
explanation: India launched the National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) in 2001 to certify organic produce. Sikkim began its phased transition to organic and was declared India's first fully organic state in 2016. Subsequently the Government launched Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) and Mission Organic Value Chain Development for North-East to scale organic farming nationally.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

items.text: Mission Organic Value Chain Development for North-East launched
Launch of National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) in 2001
Sikkim begins phased ban on chemical fertilisers and pesticides
Sikkim declared India's first fully organic state in 2016

options.text: Q → R → S → P
P → Q → R → S
S → R → Q → P
R → P → Q → S

elimination_hints.hint: MOVCDNER came after NPOP, not before
2016 declaration cannot precede 2001 NPOP
Sikkim phased ban followed NPOP launch in 2001

question_text: Arrange the following stages of designing a successful intercropping system in correct logical order from beginning to end.
explanation: Designing intercropping starts with selecting compatible crops with complementary growth habits and root systems. Spatial arrangement (row pattern, plant population) is then planned. Sowing of the two crops is done at the appropriate time relative to each other. Finally, integrated management of fertiliser, water and plant protection is carried out, and Land Equivalent Ratio is computed at harvest to evaluate the system.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

items.text: Compute LER at harvest to evaluate the intercrop
Select compatible crops with complementary growth and roots
Plan spatial arrangement and plant population
Sow crops and manage inputs in integrated manner

options.text: Q → R → S → P
P → S → R → Q
R → Q → P → S
S → P → Q → R

elimination_hints.hint: LER is computed at the end, not the beginning
Spatial planning needs crop selection first
Sowing cannot precede crop selection

question_text: State whether the following statement about Land Equivalent Ratio in intercropping evaluation is TRUE or FALSE with justification.
explanation: The statement is TRUE. An LER value greater than 1 means the intercropping system is more land-productive than sole cropping; that is, more sole-cropped land would be needed to produce the same total output. An LER of 1.0 means equal productivity, while less than 1.0 indicates intercropping is less productive than sole cropping of the components separately.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture
statement: An LER value greater than 1.0 in intercropping indicates yield advantage over sole cropping the same crops separately.

options.text: TRUE — more sole-cropped area would be needed for the same total output as the intercrop
FALSE — LER above 1 indicates intercropping has failed
FALSE — LER above 1 means weeds have outgrown both crops
FALSE — LER value cannot exceed 1.0 mathematically

elimination_hints.hint: LER above 1 indicates success, not failure
LER measures component crops, not weeds
LER can mathematically exceed 1.0 indicating yield gain

question_text: Study the comparison table of advanced cropping systems shown in the diagram and answer the question that follows about each system.
explanation: The diagram distinguishes seven cropping systems by definition, example and key benefit. Relay cropping (paira) is sowing the second crop in a standing first crop before harvest, saving time and using residual moisture. Other rows differentiate sequence, ratoon, alley, rotation, organic and precision agriculture by their distinct features and complementary advantages.
correct_answer_text: Relay cropping involves sowing a second crop in a standing first crop before its harvest.
topic: Crop Classification, Sustainable and Modern Agriculture
subject: agriculture

options.text: Ratoon cropping involves growing crops between rows of trees in alleys
Organic farming relies fully on synthetic chemical fertilisers and pesticides
Relay cropping involves sowing a second crop in standing first crop before harvest
Precision agriculture uses uniform input application across the entire field

elimination_hints.hint: That description is alley cropping, not ratoon
Organic farming excludes synthetic inputs
Precision agriculture uses site-specific not uniform inputs
